description_long: This is a historic brick church with a steeply pitched red-tiled roof and a prominent square bell tower clad in dark wood. The structure features ornate stepped gables on one end, typical of Northern European Gothic or Brick Gothic architecture. The main entrance is set into a small stone porch with an arched doorway, and the building sits on a grassy base, suggesting a landscaped churchyard. The windows are tall and narrow, with some decorative stonework, but there are no visible stained glass panels. The roof and walls show weathering, indicating age and authenticity. The church's overall form is elongated, with the nave running parallel to the ground and the bell tower rising at one end.
description: A historic brick church with a red-tiled roof, stepped gables, and a tall wooden bell tower, set on a grassy base. The structure features ornate stonework and a small arched entrance porch.
description_view_0: This view highlights the wooden bell tower, the main entrance porch, and the long red-tiled roof, with the stepped gable partially visible.
description_view_1: The stepped gable is fully visible, along with the side entrance and the length of the nave, showing more of the church's decorative brickwork.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the stepped gable and the rear of the church, with the bell tower seen in the background and the grassy base extending around the structure.
description_view_3: The wooden bell tower dominates this view, with the main entrance porch and the side of the church visible, showing the transition from brick to wood.
